```text
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## What this is
6	
7	A simplified PTT-style BBS (批踢踢 clone) served over SSH. Built with
8	`charmbracelet/wish` (SSH framework) + `bubbletea` (TUI), persisted in
9	SQLite via the pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` driver. **Vim-style navigation
10	is a deliberate product decision** — `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` work alongside arrow
11	keys and Esc/Enter on every list/menu screen. Form screens (register,
12	post compose, water-balloon compose) intentionally do *not* bind h/l so
13	they remain usable as text-edit keys.
14	
15	For the why-we-do-it-this-way notes mapping back to pttbbs internals, see
16	`docs/ptt_trace_code/`. For the testing strategy, see `docs/testing.md`.
17	
18	## Common commands
19	
20	```bash
21	make hostkey            # generate .ssh/host_ed25519 (run once before first run)
22	make run                # boots :2222, creates data/bbs.db with seed boards
23	make watch              # auto-rebuild on .go/.sql changes; SIGINT-then-restart via `air`
24	make build              # produces ./sshbbs
25	make test               # plain go test
26	make test-race          # what we treat as the CI standard
27	make cover              # per-package coverage
28	make db-reset           # rm data/bbs.db*
29	
30	# focused testing
31	go test -run TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity -race -v ./internal/store/...
32	go test -race ./internal/chat/...
33	
34	# manual two-user smoke
35	ssh new@localhost -p 2222     # password ignored — fills the in-TUI register form
36	ssh alice@localhost -p 2222   # use the password set during register
37	```
38	
39	If `make hostkey` is re-run, clients see `Host key verification failed`.
40	Fix: `ssh-keygen -R '[localhost]:2222'`.
41	
42	## Architecture — the non-obvious parts
43	
44	### SSH-user-as-login, with `new` as the register sentinel
45	`auth.ReservedUsernameNew = "new"`. The password-auth callback in
46	`internal/server/auth_middleware.go` accepts any password for SSH user
47	`new` and stashes a "register mode" flag in `ssh.Context`; the TUI
48	handler reads it and routes straight to the register screen. For any
49	other SSH username, `auth.VerifyLogin` runs bcrypt against the DB and
50	stashes the `user_id` (int64) in the context for `makeProgramHandler`
51	to load.
52	
53	This means `auth.Register` rejects the literal username `new` — that
54	constant is referenced in two places (auth + middleware) and they must
55	not drift. `auth.go` defines it once.
56	
57	### Wish uses `MiddlewareWithProgramHandler`, not `Middleware`
58	We pick the *ProgramHandler* variant deliberately
59	(`internal/server/server.go`) because we need the `*tea.Program` pointer
60	to register the session with `chat.Broker` and to `program.Send(tea.Quit())`
61	during graceful shutdown. The simpler `Middleware(handler)` form constructs
62	the program internally and hides the pointer — you can't broadcast
63	into it. Don't switch back.
64	
65	### The chat broker takes a `Sender` interface, not `*tea.Program`
66	`chat.Sender` is `interface { Send(tea.Msg) }`. `*tea.Program` satisfies
67	it naturally. The interface exists purely so unit tests can substitute
68	a recording fake (`fakeSender` in `broker_test.go`) without spinning up
69	a real bubbletea program. If you find yourself wanting to mock the
70	broker, you probably want to add a method here, not refactor.
71	
72	### Live broadcasts are "send to everyone, filter on receive"
73	For 推文 (push) updates, `screen_article_view.updatePushInput` calls
74	`broker.SendToAll(senderUID, PushAddedMsg{ArticleID, ...})`. Every
75	connected session receives the message; each article-view model
76	ignores it unless `msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID`. This is simpler than
77	tracking who's viewing what and good enough for our scale. When the
78	filter matches, we **re-fetch from DB** rather than trust the broker
79	payload — that way timestamps and `recommend_score` are always canonical.
80	
81	Water balloons (`broker.Send(toUID, ...)`) target a single user; the
82	multi-session "雙開" case fans out to every live `Session` of that user.
83	
84	### `Store.writeMu` is process-level, not row-level
85	`internal/store/store.go` exposes a single `sync.Mutex` that's grabbed
86	inside every multi-statement repo method (e.g. `PushRepo.Create` runs
87	`INSERT INTO pushes` and `UPDATE articles SET recommend_score` in one
88	transaction under the mutex). This keeps the cached score atomic from
89	the application's perspective and avoids `SQLITE_BUSY` flapping with
90	the pure-Go driver. Don't use `sql.Tx` ad-hoc inside repo methods
91	without also taking `writeMu`. The `pushes` test
92	(`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`, 50 concurrent inserts) is the
93	canary — if it ever fails under `-race`, the mutex discipline broke.
94	
95	### Migrations live under the store package, not the repo root
96	`internal/store/migrations/*.sql` is embedded with `go:embed
97	migrations/*.sql` from `internal/store/migrate.go`. `go:embed` can't
98	ascend with `..`, so the SQL files live next to the Go code that loads
99	them. Filename format `NNNN_name.sql`; `migrate.apply()` parses the
100	prefix as the version and tracks applied versions in the
101	`schema_migrations` table.
102	
103	### Data deliberately diverges from pttbbs in three places
104	1. **No recno-based article addressing** — `articles.id` (auto-increment)
105	   is opaque and never re-used. Bookmarks stay valid forever.
106	2. **bcrypt cost-12, not `crypt(3)` DES** — `auth.bcryptCost`.
107	3. **No binary shared memory / `userinfo_t` arrays** — presence is a
108	   `sync.RWMutex`-guarded `map[int64][]*Session` in `chat.Broker`.
109	
110	The full mapping (userec_t / boardheader_t / fileheader_t / `.DIR` /
111	推文 / 水球 / userinfo_t) is in `docs/ptt_trace_code/01..06_*.md`.
112	
113	## Testing layers (see `docs/testing.md` for the full doc)
114	
115	- **Layer 1**: plain `go test` for `auth` / `store` / `chat`. Most coverage lives here.
116	- **Layer 2**: direct `tea.Model.Update()` tests in `internal/tui/`. Use
117	  `package tui` (internal), not `tui_test`, to reach unexported screen
118	  constructors. Keybinding-parity tests are table-driven over
119	  `[]string{"esc","backspace","left","h"}` etc. — when adding new keys,
120	  extend the table.
121	- **No `teatest` dependency.** It's experimental (`x/exp`); we test
122	  `Update()` directly with `tea.KeyMsg{...}` synthetic input.
123	- **Always run `-race`.** Several tests (`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`,
124	  `TestBroker_Concurrency`) exist specifically as race-detector bait.
125	- **Any test needing a DB** uses `storetest.New(t)` which gives a
126	  fresh `t.TempDir()`-backed SQLite. Never share stores across tests.
127	
128	## Conventions worth preserving
129	
130	- **UTF-8 throughout, no Big5.** Use `internal/tui/runewidth.go` helpers
131	  (`PadRight`, `PadLeft`, `Truncate`, `Width`) for any list rendering
132	  that mixes ASCII and CJK — `lipgloss`'s automatic width is unreliable
133	  for double-width glyphs.
134	- **Form screens skip h/l navigation** so the keys remain available for
135	  text editing. Currently: `screen_register`, `screen_post_compose`,
136	  `screen_wb` (compose half).
137	- **NavigateMsg is the only legitimate way to swap screens.** Sub-models
138	  emit it from `Update`; `Root.navigate` is the single switch statement
139	  that constructs the new sub. When you add a new screen, add the case
140	  there and a `Screen*` constant in `messages.go`.
141	- **Window-resize forwarding**: `Root.Update` always forwards
142	  `tea.WindowSizeMsg` to the active sub before its own logic. When
143	  switching to a new sub, `Root.navigate` resends the last known size
144	  so the freshly-mounted screen lays out correctly on first render.
145	
146	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance -->
147	<!-- Snippet for the project's agent contract file (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md /
148	     similar). The bundled scripts/init.sh appends this between sentinel
149	     markers; safe to re-run. -->
150	
151	### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/`
152	
153	When the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being implemented this
154	session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have", "if I'm interested",
155	"工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add an entry to [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) using
156	the priority + effort tag schema. Do **not** create new `ROADMAP.md` /
157	`IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files — `TODO.md` is the single index.
158	
159	The bundled `scripts/todo-kanban.sh` validates the format. Run it
160	(`scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only TODO.md`) after editing so syntax
161	drift is caught immediately.
162	
163	#### Three ways to add a TODO entry (preferred order)
164	
165	1. **Structured CLI — `scripts/add-todo.sh`** (default):
166	
167	   ```
168	   scripts/add-todo.sh --priority P3 --effort M \
169	     --title "Title" --description "Description"
170	   ```
171	
172	   Inserts a canonically-formatted line into the right `## P*` lane and
173	   re-runs the validator. Add `--backlog` to also scaffold
174	   `backlog/<slug>.md` from the bundled template.
175	
176	2. **Quick capture — `backlog/inbox.md`** (when priority/effort unclear):
177	
178	   ```
179	   echo "- maybe add docs versioning with mike" >> backlog/inbox.md
180	   ```
181	
182	   When the user asks "sweep the inbox", run
183	   `scripts/sweep-inbox.sh`. It prompts for the missing fields per loose
184	   line and calls `add-todo.sh`. Use `--batch` for non-interactive runs
185	   that only formalize lines with parseable `key=value` pairs.
186	
187	3. **Direct edit of `TODO.md`** — fine if the format is fresh; run
188	   `scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only` afterwards.
189	
190	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` companion doc when the item meets any of:
191	
192	- carries a `P?` tag (record what was tried so it doesn't need re-investigation)
193	- captures a paused troubleshooting session that you intend to fix later
194	  (preserve the error trace + root cause analysis before context evaporates)
195	- weighs multiple options (record trade-offs, not only the winner)
196	- is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
197	
198	`[S]` items rarely need a backlog doc — a file path in the `TODO.md` line is
199	usually enough. See [`backlog/README.md`](backlog/README.md) for the full
200	template and "when to add a doc" rules.
201	
202	When implementing a `TODO.md` item, in the same commit:
203	
204	1. Run `scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<what shipped>"`
205	   to move the entry into `## Done` with the dated syntax and re-validate.
206	2. Mark the corresponding `backlog/<slug>.md` (if any) `Status: shipped`
207	   and keep it as a historical record (don't delete — future-you may
208	   revisit adjacent decisions).
209	
210	`backlog/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A); it
211	is repo metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
212	
213	### Past pitfalls → `pitfalls/`
214	
215	When you spend more than ~15 minutes debugging something that wasn't
216	googleable and the fix is non-obvious, write a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
217	capturing:
218	
219	1. **Verbatim symptom** — copy-paste error messages exactly, do not
220	   paraphrase (preserves grep-ability for future-you / future agent)
221	2. **Root cause** — why this happens (with source / docs / upstream issue link)
222	3. **Workaround** — copy-pasteable commands or config diff
223	4. **Prevention** — how to avoid stepping on this again
224	
225	Title the doc by the **symptom**, not the root cause (you'll search by what
226	you're seeing, not by what you eventually learned). See
227	[`pitfalls/README.md`](pitfalls/README.md) for the full template and
228	when-to-add rules.
229	
230	**Pitfall vs Hard invariant**: a pitfall *graduates* to a Hard invariant in
231	this file when it (a) recurs across machines/agents/sessions despite being
232	documented, (b) silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
233	enough that "remember to do X" isn't safe. When graduating, leave the
234	`pitfalls/<slug>.md` as historical record and link to it from the new
235	invariant.
236	
237	`pitfalls/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A) and
238	**not** auto-redacted; review for secrets before committing.
239	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance --> (end)
240

1	package store

3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Board struct {
11		ID          int64
12		Name        string
13		Title       string
14		Description string
15		BM          string
16		Attr        int64
17		CreatedAt   time.Time
18		// Banner is per-board ANSI/ASCII-art text. Empty = no banner (column
19		// NULL in DB). Edited by mod+ via the banner-edit screen; seeded from
20		// internal/seed/banners on first boot.
21		Banner string
22	}
23	
24	type BoardRepo struct{ s *Store }
25	
26	var ErrBoardNotFound = errors.New("board not found")
27	
28	const boardColumns = `id, name, title, description, bm, attr, created_at, banner`
29	
30	func scanBoard(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Board, error) {
31		b := &Board{}
32		var banner sql.NullString
33		if err := row.Scan(&b.ID, &b.Name, &b.Title, &b.Description, &b.BM, &b.Attr, &b.CreatedAt, &banner); err != nil {
34			return b, err
35		}
36		if banner.Valid {
37			b.Banner = banner.String
38		}
39		return b, nil
40	}
41	
42	func (r *BoardRepo) List(ctx context.Context) ([]*Board, error) {
43		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
44			`SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards ORDER BY name COLLATE NOCASE`)
45		if err != nil {
46			return nil, err
47		}
48		defer rows.Close()
49		var out []*Board
50		for rows.Next() {
51			b, err := scanBoard(rows)
52			if err != nil {
53				return nil, err
54			}
55			out = append(out, b)
56		}
57		return out, rows.Err()
58	}
59	
60	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Board, error) {
61		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, id)
62		b, err := scanBoard(row)
63		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
64			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
65		}
66		return b, err
67	}
68	
69	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByName(ctx context.Context, name string) (*Board, error) {
70		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE name = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, name)
71		b, err := scanBoard(row)
72		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
73			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
74		}
75		return b, err
76	}
77	
78	// SeedDefaults is a belt-and-braces complement to migration 0002. It runs
79	// every startup; INSERT OR IGNORE makes it a no-op once the rows exist.
80	func (r *BoardRepo) SeedDefaults(ctx context.Context) error {
81		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
82		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
83		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `
84			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO boards (name, title, description) VALUES
85				('Welcome',  '歡迎來到 SSH-BBS', 'System welcome and announcements'),
86				('Test',     '測試板',           'Try things here. Posts may be cleared periodically.'),
87				('ChitChat', '閒聊',             'General chat. Be kind.')
88		`)
89		return err
90	}
91	
92	// UpdateBanner sets the banner column on a board. Permission: requester
93	// must have a role at-or-above mod (the global mod role; per-board BM is
94	// not consulted). Returns ErrBoardNotFound if the board is missing,
95	// ErrPermissionDenied if the requester is unauthorized.
96	//
97	// Mirrors ArticleRepo.Update's permission shape; takes writeMu for the
98	// same reason (single-statement write is fine, but keeping the lock
99	// discipline consistent across repos is the point).
100	func (r *BoardRepo) UpdateBanner(ctx context.Context, boardID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, banner string) error {
101		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
102			return ErrPermissionDenied
103		}
104		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
105		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
106		var exists int64
107		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT id FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, boardID).Scan(&exists)
108		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
109			return ErrBoardNotFound
110		}
111		if err != nil {
112			return err
113		}
114		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `UPDATE boards SET banner = ? WHERE id = ?`, banner, boardID)
115		return err
116	}

```text
1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7	
8		"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
11	)
12	
13	const (
14		// GuestPasswordSentinel is stored as the bcrypt hash for the guest
15		// user. It is deliberately not a valid bcrypt prefix, so even if the
16		// SSH password callback ever falls through to bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword
17		// for guest, the comparison fails. The SSH callback short-circuits guest
18		// before reaching VerifyLogin, so this is belt-and-suspenders.
19		GuestPasswordSentinel = "!"
20	
21		// DefaultAdminPassword is the password baked in when the operator
22		// does not pass -admin-password. Combined with must_change_password=1
23		// and the loopback-only restriction in MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked,
24		// the operator must rotate it locally before the admin account becomes
25		// reachable from the network.
26		DefaultAdminPassword = "admin"
27	)
28	
29	// SeedOpts controls the bootstrap seed.
30	type SeedOpts struct {
31		// AdminPassword overrides DefaultAdminPassword on first seed.
32		// Empty string = use the baked default. Ignored if admin already exists.
33		AdminPassword string
34	}
35	
36	// SeedSystemAccounts creates the bootstrap admin and guest accounts if
37	// they do not exist. Idempotent — calling on a populated DB is a no-op.
38	// Lives in auth (not store) because it owns the bcrypt cost decision.
39	func SeedSystemAccounts(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, opts SeedOpts) error {
40		if err := seedAdmin(ctx, st, opts); err != nil {
41			return fmt.Errorf("seed admin: %w", err)
42		}
43		if err := seedGuest(ctx, st); err != nil {
44			return fmt.Errorf("seed guest: %w", err)
45		}
46		return nil
47	}
48	
49	func seedAdmin(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, opts SeedOpts) error {
50		if _, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, ReservedUsernameAdmin); err == nil {
51			return nil
52		} else if !errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
53			return err
54		}
55		pw := opts.AdminPassword
56		if pw == "" {
57			pw = DefaultAdminPassword
58		}
59		hash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(pw), bcryptCost)
60		if err != nil {
61			return err
62		}
63		_, err = st.Users().InsertSystemAccount(
64			ctx, ReservedUsernameAdmin, string(hash), store.RoleAdmin, true,
65		)
66		return err
67	}
68	
69	func seedGuest(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store) error {
70		if _, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, ReservedUsernameGuest); err == nil {
71			return nil
72		} else if !errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
73			return err
74		}
75		_, err := st.Users().InsertSystemAccount(
76			ctx, ReservedUsernameGuest, GuestPasswordSentinel, store.RoleGuest, false,
77		)
78		return err
79	}
80

Perfect! Now let me look at the articles store to see all the authorization checks:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-05-06 08:15:00Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/articles.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Article struct {
11		ID             int64
12		BoardID        int64
13		AuthorID       int64
14		AuthorUserID   string
15		Title          string
16		Body           string
17		RecommendScore int64
18		Filemode       int64
19		CreatedAt      time.Time
20		UpdatedAt      sql.NullTime
21		PinnedAt       sql.NullTime
22	}
23	
24	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
25	
26	var (
27		ErrArticleNotFound  = errors.New("article not found")
28		ErrPermissionDenied = errors.New("permission denied")
29	)
30	
31	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
32		recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at, pinned_at`
33	
34	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
35		a := &Article{}
36		err := row.Scan(
37			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
38			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt, &a.PinnedAt,
39		)
40		return a, err
41	}
42	
43	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
44		if limit <= 0 {
45			limit = 50
46		}
47		// Pinned articles surface first ((pinned_at IS NULL) sorts 0 before 1);
48		// within each group, newest first by created_at then id (the latter
49		// disambiguates rows that share a second-resolution timestamp).
50		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
51			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ?
52			 ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL), created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
53			boardID, limit,
54		)
55		if err != nil {
56			return nil, err
57		}
58		defer rows.Close()
59		var out []*Article
60		for rows.Next() {
61			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
62			if err != nil {
63				return nil, err
64			}
65			out = append(out, a)
66		}
67		return out, rows.Err()
68	}
69	
70	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
71		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
72		a, err := scanArticle(row)
73		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
74			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
75		}
76		return a, err
77	}
78	
79	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
80	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
81	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
82	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
83	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
84		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
85			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
86			boardID, articleID,
87		)
88		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
89			return 0, 0, scanErr
90		}
91		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
92			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
93			boardID, articleID,
94		)
95		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
96			return 0, 0, scanErr
97		}
98		return prev, next, nil
99	}
100	
101	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
102		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
103		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
104		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
105			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
106			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
107		)
108		if err != nil {
109			return nil, err
110		}
111		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
112		if err != nil {
113			return nil, err
114		}
115		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
116		return scanArticle(row)
117	}
118	
119	// Delete hard-deletes the article. Permission: requester must be the author
120	// OR have a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row
121	// is gone, ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
122	// Cascades to pushes via the FK on pushes.article_id.
123	func (r *ArticleRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
124		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
125		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
126		var authorID int64
127		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
128			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
129		).Scan(&authorID)
130		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
131			return ErrArticleNotFound
132		}
133		if err != nil {
134			return err
135		}
136		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
137			return ErrPermissionDenied
138		}
139		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID)
140		return err
141	}
142	
143	// Update mutates the article's title and body and sets updated_at to now.
144	// Permission mirrors Delete: requester must be the author OR have a role
145	// at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound / ErrPermissionDenied. Score,
146	// board, and author_id are intentionally not editable here — those would be
147	// separate concerns (move-board / re-attribute) outside the user-facing
148	// edit flow.
149	func (r *ArticleRepo) Update(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, newTitle, newBody string) error {
150		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
151		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
152		var authorID int64
153		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
154			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
155		).Scan(&authorID)
156		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
157			return ErrArticleNotFound
158		}
159		if err != nil {
160			return err
161		}
162		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
163			return ErrPermissionDenied
164		}
165		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
166			`UPDATE articles SET title = ?, body = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
167			newTitle, newBody, articleID,
168		)
169		return err
170	}
171	
172	// SetPinned pins (pinned=true) or unpins (pinned=false) an article. Permission
173	// shape mirrors BoardRepo.UpdateBanner: requesterRole must be at-or-above
174	// mod. Pinning is a moderation action — unlike Update/Delete it does NOT
175	// admit the article author. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row is gone,
176	// ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
177	//
178	// Multi-pin per board is supported: the call simply sets pinned_at; nothing
179	// rejects a second pinned row on the same board.
180	func (r *ArticleRepo) SetPinned(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, pinned bool) error {
181		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
182			return ErrPermissionDenied
183		}
184		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
185		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
186		var exists int64
187		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
188			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
189		).Scan(&exists)
190		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
191			return ErrArticleNotFound
192		}
193		if err != nil {
194			return err
195		}
196		if pinned {
197			_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
198				`UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
199			)
200		} else {
201			_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
202				`UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = NULL WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
203			)
204		}
205		return err
206	}
207
```

1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"net"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
8	)
9	
10	// MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked reports whether an SSH login attempt
11	// for an admin account whose password is still the bootstrap default
12	// should be refused because the connection came from outside the loopback
13	// range. The intent: if the binary is published with the baked default
14	// `admin`/`admin` and the operator never rotated it, a remote attacker
15	// can't crack it just by reading the source. Local administration via
16	// `ssh admin@localhost` (the documented bootstrap path) still works.
17	//
18	// The host parameter is the SSH session's RemoteAddr().String() — typically
19	// "127.0.0.1:1234" or "[::1]:5678". Unparseable hosts are treated as
20	// non-loopback (fail-closed).
21	func MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked(u *store.User, host string) bool {
22		if u == nil {
23			return false
24		}
25		if u.Role != store.RoleAdmin {
26			return false
27		}
28		if !u.MustChangePassword {
29			return false
30		}
31		return !isLoopbackHost(host)
32	}
33	
34	func isLoopbackHost(host string) bool {
35		if host == "" {
36			return false
37		}
38		// Strip the port if present. SplitHostPort fails on bare hosts like
39		// "127.0.0.1" — fall back to using the raw string.
40		h, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(host)
41		if err != nil {
42			h = host
43		}
44		// Trim IPv6 brackets if SplitHostPort didn't (it normally does).
45		h = strings.TrimPrefix(h, "[")
46		h = strings.TrimSuffix(h, "]")
47		ip := net.ParseIP(h)
48		if ip == nil {
49			// Hostname like "localhost". Conservatively accept the literal
50			// "localhost" string; anything else is non-loopback.
51			return strings.EqualFold(h, "localhost")
52		}
53		return ip.IsLoopback()
54	}
55
```

42	// renderBody runs the article body through glamour with word-wrap set
43	// to the current viewport.
44	func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
45		if m.article == nil {
46			m.rendered = ""
47			m.renderedWidth = 0
48			return
49		}
50		width := m.width - 4

61		if err != nil {

63		}

78	// canDeleteArticle returns true if the current user may delete the loaded
79	// article (author, or mod-or-above). Used both as a permission gate at
80	// the key handler and to decide whether to render the help-line hint.
81	func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
82		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
83			return false
84		}
85		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
86			return true
87		}

89	}

91	// canDeletePush mirrors canDeleteArticle for the push at index idx.
92	func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
93		if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
94			return false
95		}
96		if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
97			return true
98		}
99		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
100	}

## Summary: Article + Push Subsystem Map

### 1. **Articles Table Schema**
**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql` (lines 33–43)

Columns:
- `id` (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT) — opaque, never reused
- `board_id` (INTEGER, FK to boards)
- `author_id` (INTEGER, FK to users)
- `author_userid` (TEXT, denormalized)
- `title` (TEXT)
- `body` (TEXT)
- `recommend_score` (INTEGER, cached sum: push=+1, boo=−1, arrow=0)
- `filemode` (INTEGER, pttbbs analog)
- `created_at` (DATETIME)

**Migrations touching articles:**
1. `0001_init.sql` — initial table + index on `(board_id, created_at DESC)`
2. `0005_articles_updated_at.sql` — adds `updated_at` DATETIME (null until first edit)
3. `0007_articles_pinned_at.sql` — adds `pinned_at` DATETIME (null = unpinned; used for board rules) + index on `(board_id, pinned_at)`

**Article struct:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/articles.go` (lines 10–22)
```go
type Article struct {
    ID             int64
    BoardID        int64
    AuthorID       int64
    AuthorUserID   string
    Title          string
    Body           string
    RecommendScore int64
    Filemode       int64
    CreatedAt      time.Time
    UpdatedAt      sql.NullTime  // 0005 migration
    PinnedAt       sql.NullTime  // 0007 migration
}
```

---

### 2. **Pushes Table Schema & Types**
**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql` (lines 46–54)

Columns:
- `id` (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT)
- `article_id` (INTEGER, FK to articles, cascades on delete)
- `user_id` (INTEGER, FK to users)
- `user_userid` (TEXT, denormalized)
- `kind` (TEXT CHECK IN ('push', 'boo', 'arrow')) — string-stored, checked at DB level
- `body` (TEXT, optional comment)
- `created_at` (DATETIME)

**Push kind constants:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/pushes.go` (lines 15–21)
```go
type PushKind string

const (
    PushKindPush  PushKind = "push"  // 推 (+1)
    PushKindBoo   PushKind = "boo"   // 噓 (-1)
    PushKindArrow PushKind = "arrow" // → (0)
)
```

**Push struct:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/pushes.go` (lines 35–43)
```go
type Push struct {
    ID         int64
    ArticleID  int64
    UserID     int64
    UserUserID string
    Kind       PushKind
    Body       string
    CreatedAt  time.Time
}
```

---

### 3. **Push Creation Path: `PushRepo.Create`**
**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/pushes.go` (lines 97–136)

**Signature:**
```go
func (r *PushRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, articleID, userID int64, userUserID string, 
    kind PushKind, body string) (*Push, error)
```

**What it does:**
1. Computes `delta` from `scoreDelta(kind)`: +1 for push, −1 for boo, 0 for arrow
2. **Takes `r.s.writeMu.Lock()`** — process-level mutex (documented in CLAUDE.md lines 84–93)
3. Begins SQLite transaction (`tx := r.s.db.BeginTx(...)`)
4. Inserts the push row
5. **If delta ≠ 0**, runs `UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score + ? WHERE id = ?`
6. Commits transaction
7. Refetches and returns the canonical push row

**Critical insight:** The `writeMu` + single transaction ensures atomicity for concurrent pushes on the same article. The `recommend_score` is cached, not computed on-the-fly.

---

### 4. **Push Input UI Flow in Article View**
**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go`

**Push type selection (lines 215–229):**
- User presses `+` → `openPush(store.PushKindPush)` sets `m.pushKind` and enters input mode
- User presses `-` → `openPush(store.PushKindBoo)`
- User presses `=` → `openPush(store.PushKindArrow)`

**Push input handler: `updatePushInput` (lines 373–413):**
- On **Esc**: cancel, blur input
- On **Enter**:
  1. Trim body text
  2. Validate: comment required for 推/噓 (arrow can be empty)
  3. Call `m.deps.Store.Pushes().Create(...)` with `m.article.ID`, `u.ID`, `u.UserID`, `m.pushKind`, body
  4. On success:
     - Append locally to `m.pushes` for immediate UI
     - Refetch article from DB (to get canonical `recommend_score`)
     - Exit input mode
     - **Broadcast:** `m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{ArticleID, UserUserID, Kind, Body})`
  5. All connected sessions receive the message; article-view models filter by `ArticleID` and refetch from DB

---

### 5. **Article Rendering & Push Display**
**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go` (lines 415–523)

**Article body rendering:**
- Title, date, score displayed (lines 429–431)
- Body rendered via `glamour` markdown renderer (cached at `m.rendered` to avoid recomputation on every keystroke)
- Scroll position `m.scroll` tracks viewport

**Push list rendering (lines 450–470):**
- Grouped under "── 推文 (N) ──" header
- Each push: `glyph user_id comment timestamp`
- `renderPushKind(p.Kind)` converts `PushKind` to styled glyph: `推` / `噓` / `→`
- Push cursor (`m.pushCursor`) highlights the currently selected push for deletion

---

### 6. **Admin/Moderation Code & Roles**
**User roles:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/role.go` (lines 9–20)
```go
const (
    RoleGuest Role = "guest" // read-only
    RoleUser  Role = "user"  // default registered
    RoleMod   Role = "mod"   // can delete any article/push
    RoleAdmin Role = "admin" // can promote/demote others
)
```

**Rank check:** `Role.AtLeast(min Role) bool` — returns true if requester is at or above min.

**User table:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0004_add_user_roles.sql` (lines 1–10)
- Adds `role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user'` with CHECK constraint
- Adds `must_change_password INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0` (for bootstrapped admin)
- Creates index on `(role)`

**User struct:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/users.go` (lines 10–24)
```go
type User struct {
    ID                 int64
    UserID             string
    Role               Role
    MustChangePassword bool
    // ...other fields
}
```

**Permission gates in article view:**
- `canDeleteArticle()` (lines 81–89): author OR role ≥ mod
- `canDeletePush()` (lines 92–100): push author OR role ≥ mod
- `canPush()` (lines 104–106): not guest
- `canEditArticle()` (lines 110–118): author OR role ≥ mod

### 7. **Board Ownership / Moderation**
**Boards table:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql` (lines 23–31)
- `bm` (TEXT) — boardmaster(s), comma-separated userids (pttbbs analog, currently not enforced)
- `attr` (INTEGER) — board attributes (pttbbs flags, not used in current TUI gates)

type Board struct {
    ID          int64
    Name        string
    Title       string
    Description string
    BM          string        // denormalized, not enforced
    Attr        int64
    CreatedAt   time.Time

**Current implementation:** Board moderation uses **global roles only** (no per-board BM enforcement). Banner updates require `role ≥ mod` (lines 100–101 in boards.go).

No mention of moderation flags at creation time in the current schema. Article metadata is minimal: title, body, and that's it.

---

## Planning Edits for Moderation Features

**Key gates you'll need to add:**

1. **Schema changes:**
   - Add `is_locked` BOOLEAN to articles (blocks all push types)
   - Add `arrow_only` BOOLEAN to articles (blocks push/boo, allows arrow only)

2. **Permission checks in `PushRepo.Create()`:**
   - Before inserting push, fetch article and check its `is_locked` and `arrow_only` flags
   - Reject push if locked; reject push/boo if arrow-only

Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me create a comprehensive report:

## Identity & Authorization Architecture Report

### 1. **Users Table Schema**
**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql` (lines 9–21) + `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0004_add_user_roles.sql` (lines 7–10)

**User struct** (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/users.go`, lines 10–24):
- `ID` (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY)
- `UserID` (TEXT UNIQUE) — SSH username (case-insensitive)
- `PasswordHash` (TEXT) — bcrypt
- `Nickname` (TEXT)
- `RealName` (TEXT)
- `Email` (TEXT)
- `NumLogins` (INTEGER)
- `NumPosts` (INTEGER)
- `LastLoginAt` (DATETIME)
- `LastHost` (TEXT)
- `CreatedAt` (DATETIME)
- **`Role` (TEXT)** — Four-tier enum: `guest` < `user` < `mod` < `admin`
- **`MustChangePassword` (INTEGER / bool)** — Forces password rotation for bootstrapped admin on first login

### 2. **Current User in TUI**
**Deps struct** (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/root.go`):
```go
type Deps struct {
    Store              *store.Store
    Broker             *chat.Broker // nil during register
    User               *store.User  // nil iff IsRegister / guest
    IsRegister         bool
    MustChangePassword bool
}
```

**Flow**: `auth.VerifyLogin()` returns the full `*store.User` object. The SSH middleware stashes it in `ssh.Context`, which is passed into TUI screens as `Deps.User`. Screens like `articleViewModel` (line 58 in `screen_article_view.go`) receive `deps Deps` and access `deps.User` directly (e.g., line 85: `deps.User.ID == article.AuthorID`, line 88: `deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)`). Guest login short-circuits to a pre-seeded "guest" user with `Role: RoleGuest` and does not invoke `NoteLogin` (auth.go line 138).

### 3. **Boards Table Schema**
**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql` (lines 23–31)

**Board struct** (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/boards.go`, lines 10–22):
- `ID` (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY)
- `Name` (TEXT UNIQUE)
- `Title` (TEXT)
- `Description` (TEXT)
- **`BM` (TEXT)** — Comma-sep list (legacy pttbbs field; not currently used for authorization)
- `Attr` (INTEGER) — Bitfield (legacy)
- `CreatedAt` (DATETIME)
- **`Banner` (TEXT)** — Per-board ANSI/ASCII art; editable by mod+

**Note**: No per-board moderators list or owner field. Global `Role` is used; the `BM` column is a denormalized legacy field (unused). Boards permissions check only global role (e.g., `UpdateBanner` at line 100–102: `!requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod)` returns `ErrPermissionDenied`).

### 4. **Authorization Checks Across the Codebase**

| Action | Gate | Location |
|--------|------|----------|
| **Delete article** | Author OR `mod+` | `store/articles.go:123–141` — checks `authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod)` |
| **Edit article** (title/body) | Author OR `mod+` | `store/articles.go:149–170` — same gate as Delete |
| **Pin article** | `mod+` only (NOT author) | `store/articles.go:180–206` — `!requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod)` alone |
| **Delete push/comment** | Push author OR `mod+` | `store/pushes.go:163–164` — mirrors article delete |
| **Edit board banner** | `mod+` | `store/boards.go:100–102` — `!requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod)` |
| **Promote/demote user** | `admin` | TUI `screen_admin_users.go` — only admins see the admin menu (checked in `root.go` line ~180: `deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin`) |
| **View admin menu** | `admin` | `root.go` — conditional render of admin panel |
| **Bootstrap admin** | Remote-blocked until password changed | `auth/policy.go:21–32` — `MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked()` prevents remote logins with default password |

**UI gates**:
- Article/push delete buttons rendered conditionally via `canDeleteArticle()` / `canDeletePush()` (screen_article_view.go, lines 81–100)
- Board banner edit button gated at screen_board_view.go lines 231–238

### 5. **Auth Package Exports**
**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/auth/`

**Functions** (no Role type in auth; Role lives in store):
- `IsReservedUsername(s string) bool` — rejects "new", "guest", "admin"
- `ValidatePassword(password string) error` — checks 6–128 chars
- `HashPassword(password string) (string, error)` — bcrypt cost 12
- `VerifyPasswordHash(hash, password string) error` — bcrypt compare
- `Register(ctx, st, userID, password, nickname, email)` — creates user with `Role: user` (default)
- `VerifyLogin(ctx, st, userID, password, host)` — authenticates and updates last_login_at
- `SeedSystemAccounts(ctx, st, opts SeedOpts)` — creates admin + guest on first boot
- `MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked(u, host)` — loopback-check for admin password rotation

**Types**: Only `SeedOpts` (controls admin password override). `Role` type is in `store/role.go` (not auth).

### 6. **Seed Data**
**Admin account** (`auth/seed.go`, lines 49–67):
- `UserID: "admin"`
- `Role: "admin"`
- `MustChangePassword: 1` (forces rotation before remote access)
- Password: `opts.AdminPassword` (default `"admin"`) hashed with bcrypt cost 12

**Guest account** (`auth/seed.go`, lines 69–79):
- `UserID: "guest"`
- `Role: "guest"`
- `PasswordHash: "!"` (sentinel; no login validation)
- Shared spectator account; `NoteLogin` skipped (no stat updates)

**Boards** (`store/migrations/0002_seed_boards.sql`):
- `Welcome` — 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
- `Test` — 測試板
- `ChitChat` — 閒聊

### 7. **Register Flow**
**Function**: `auth.Register()` (`auth.go`, lines 82–117)

**Input validation**:
- `userID`: 3–12 chars, starts with letter, alphanumeric + underscore only
- `password`: 6–128 chars (validated via `ValidatePassword`)
- `nickname`: max 32 runes
- `email`: max 128 chars

**Rejection criteria**:
- Reserved names (new, guest, admin)
- Invalid userID pattern
- Password length

**Output**: New user created with hardcoded `Role: user` (line 52 in `store/users.go:Create` uses default role='user' from migration).

---

**Summary**: The system has a complete role-based authorization layer (`guest` → `user` → `mod` → `admin`) stored per-user, with gates on article/push edit+delete, board banner edit, and user promotion. No per-board moderator list exists; the `BM` field is legacy. Admin is bootstrapped with forced password rotation. Guest is a shared read-only account.

18		width   int

23		// rendered is the article body after running through glamour. We cache

42	// renderBody runs the article body through glamour with word-wrap set
43	// to the current viewport.
44	func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
45		if m.article == nil {
46			m.rendered = ""
47			m.renderedWidth = 0
48			return
49		}
50		width := m.width - 4
51		if width < 40 {
52			width = glamourFallbackWidth
53		}
54		m.rendered = renderMarkdown(m.article.Body, width)
55		m.renderedWidth = width
56	}
57	
58	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
59		ctx := context.Background()
60		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
61		if err != nil {
62			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
63		}
64		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
65	
66		ti := textinput.New()
67		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
68		ti.CharLimit = 80
69		ti.Width = 60
70	
71		m := articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
72		// Render with the fallback width so the body is readable even before
73		// the first WindowSizeMsg arrives — Update re-renders on resize.
74		m.renderBody()
75		return m
76	}
77	
78	// canDeleteArticle returns true if the current user may delete the loaded
79	// article (author, or mod-or-above). Used both as a permission gate at
80	// the key handler and to decide whether to render the help-line hint.
81	func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
82		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
83			return false
84		}
85		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
86			return true
87		}
88		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
89	}
90	
91	// canDeletePush mirrors canDeleteArticle for the push at index idx.
92	func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
93		if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
94			return false
95		}
96		if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
97			return true
98		}
99		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
100	}
101	
102	// canPush returns true if the current user is allowed to add new pushes.
103	// Guests can read but not write.
104	func (m articleViewModel) canPush() bool {
105		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest
106	}
107	
108	// canEditArticle mirrors canDeleteArticle: the author may edit their own
109	// post, mods and admins may edit anyone's.
110	func (m articleViewModel) canEditArticle() bool {
111		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
112			return false
113		}
114		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
115			return true
116		}
117		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
118	}
119	
120	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
121	
122	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
123		switch msg := msg.(type) {
124		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
125			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
126			// Only re-render when the width target actually changes — height
127			// changes don't affect glamour wrap.
128			newWidth := max(msg.Width-4, 40)
129			if newWidth != m.renderedWidth {
130				m.renderBody()
131			}
132			return m, nil
133	
134		case PushAddedMsg:
135			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
136				ctx := context.Background()
137				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
138				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
139				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
140					m.pushes = pushes
141				}
142				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
143					m.article = a
144				}
145			}
146	
147		case ArticleUpdatedMsg:
148			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
149				// Edit happened in another session — refetch the canonical
150				// title/body so we render the new text.
151				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), m.article.ID); err == nil {
152					m.article = a
153					m.renderBody()
154					// Reset scroll if the new body is shorter than where we
155					// were parked.
156					if m.scroll > m.bodyLineCount() {
157						m.scroll = 0
158					}
159				}
160			}
161			return m, nil
162	
163		case tea.KeyMsg:
164			if m.pushing {
165				return m.updatePushInput(msg)
166			}
167			if m.pendingDelete {
168				return m.updateDeleteConfirm(msg)
169			}
170			switch msg.String() {
171			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
172				boardID := int64(0)
173				if m.article != nil {
174					boardID = m.article.BoardID
175				}
176				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
177			case "Q":
178				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
179			case "up", "k":
180				if m.scroll > 0 {
181					m.scroll--
182				}
183			case "down", "j":
184				m.scroll++
185			case "pgup", "b":
186				m.scroll = max(0, m.scroll-10)
187			case "pgdown", " ":
188				m.scroll += 10
189			case "g":
190				m.scroll = 0
191			case "G":
192				m.scroll = max(0, m.bodyLineCount()-m.viewportLines())
193			case "[":
194				if m.article == nil {
195					return m, nil
196				}
197				prev, _, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
198				if err != nil || prev == 0 {
199					return m, nil
200				}
201				return m, func() tea.Msg {
202					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: prev, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
203				}
204			case "]":
205				if m.article == nil {
206					return m, nil
207				}
208				_, next, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
209				if err != nil || next == 0 {
210					return m, nil
211				}
212				return m, func() tea.Msg {
213					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: next, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
214				}
215			case "+":
216				if !m.canPush() {
217					return m, nil
218				}
219				return m.openPush(store.PushKindPush), nil
220			case "-":
221				if !m.canPush() {
222					return m, nil
223				}
224				return m.openPush(store.PushKindBoo), nil
225			case "=":
226				if !m.canPush() {
227					return m, nil
228				}
229				return m.openPush(store.PushKindArrow), nil
230			case "p":
231				// Advance the push cursor. -1 → 0 → ... → len-1 → -1 (cycles
232				// back to "no selection, D targets the article").
233				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
234					return m, nil
235				}
236				m.pushCursor++
237				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
238					m.pushCursor = -1
239				}
240				m.err = ""

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Article struct {
11		ID             int64
12		BoardID        int64
13		AuthorID       int64
14		AuthorUserID   string
15		Title          string
16		Body           string
17		RecommendScore int64
18		Filemode       int64
19		CreatedAt      time.Time
20		UpdatedAt      sql.NullTime
21		PinnedAt       sql.NullTime
22	}
23	
24	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
25	
26	var (
27		ErrArticleNotFound  = errors.New("article not found")
28		ErrPermissionDenied = errors.New("permission denied")
29	)
30	
31	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
32		recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at, pinned_at`
33	
34	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
35		a := &Article{}
36		err := row.Scan(
37			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
38			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt, &a.PinnedAt,
39		)
40		return a, err
41	}
42	
43	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
44		if limit <= 0 {
45			limit = 50
46		}
47		// Pinned articles surface first ((pinned_at IS NULL) sorts 0 before 1);
48		// within each group, newest first by created_at then id (the latter
49		// disambiguates rows that share a second-resolution timestamp).
50		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
51			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ?
52			 ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL), created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
53			boardID, limit,
54		)
55		if err != nil {
56			return nil, err
57		}
58		defer rows.Close()
59		var out []*Article
60		for rows.Next() {
61			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
62			if err != nil {
63				return nil, err
64			}
65			out = append(out, a)
66		}
67		return out, rows.Err()
68	}
69	
70	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
71		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
72		a, err := scanArticle(row)
73		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
74			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
75		}
76		return a, err
77	}
78	
79	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
80	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
81	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
82	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
83	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
84		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
85			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
86			boardID, articleID,
87		)
88		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
89			return 0, 0, scanErr
90		}
91		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
92			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
93			boardID, articleID,
94		)
95		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
96			return 0, 0, scanErr
97		}
98		return prev, next, nil
99	}
100	
101	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
102		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
103		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
104		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
105			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
106			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
107		)
108		if err != nil {
109			return nil, err
110		}
111		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
112		if err != nil {
113			return nil, err
114		}
115		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
116		return scanArticle(row)
117	}
118	
119	// Delete hard-deletes the article. Permission: requester must be the author
120	// OR have a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row
121	// is gone, ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
122	// Cascades to pushes via the FK on pushes.article_id.
123	func (r *ArticleRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
124		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
125		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
126		var authorID int64
127		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
128			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
129		).Scan(&authorID)
130		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
131			return ErrArticleNotFound
132		}
133		if err != nil {
134			return err
135		}
136		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
137			return ErrPermissionDenied
138		}
139		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID)
140		return err
141	}
142	
143	// Update mutates the article's title and body and sets updated_at to now.
144	// Permission mirrors Delete: requester must be the author OR have a role
145	// at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound / ErrPermissionDenied. Score,
146	// board, and author_id are intentionally not editable here — those would be
147	// separate concerns (move-board / re-attribute) outside the user-facing
148	// edit flow.
149	func (r *ArticleRepo) Update(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, newTitle, newBody string) error {
150		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
151		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
152		var authorID int64
153		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
154			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
155		).Scan(&authorID)
156		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
157			return ErrArticleNotFound
158		}
159		if err != nil {
160			return err
161		}
162		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
163			return ErrPermissionDenied
164		}
165		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
166			`UPDATE articles SET title = ?, body = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
167			newTitle, newBody, articleID,
168		)
169		return err
170	}
171	
172	// SetPinned pins (pinned=true) or unpins (pinned=false) an article. Permission
173	// shape mirrors BoardRepo.UpdateBanner: requesterRole must be at-or-above
174	// mod. Pinning is a moderation action — unlike Update/Delete it does NOT
175	// admit the article author. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row is gone,
176	// ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
177	//
178	// Multi-pin per board is supported: the call simply sets pinned_at; nothing
179	// rejects a second pinned row on the same board.
180	func (r *ArticleRepo) SetPinned(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, pinned bool) error {
181		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
182			return ErrPermissionDenied
183		}
184		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
185		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
186		var exists int64
187		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
188			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
189		).Scan(&exists)
190		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
191			return ErrArticleNotFound
192		}
193		if err != nil {
194			return err
195		}
196		if pinned {
197			_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
198				`UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
199			)
200		} else {
201			_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
202				`UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = NULL WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
203			)
204		}
205		return err
206	}
207

 `./internal/store/pushes.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// ErrPushNotFound is returned by PushRepo lookups (Delete, future GetByID)
12	// when the row is missing. ErrPermissionDenied is shared with articles.
13	var ErrPushNotFound = errors.New("push not found")
14	
15	type PushKind string
16	
17	const (
18		PushKindPush  PushKind = "push"  // 推 (+1)
19		PushKindBoo   PushKind = "boo"   // 噓 (-1)
20		PushKindArrow PushKind = "arrow" // → (0)
21	)
22	
23	func (k PushKind) Glyph() string {
24		switch k {
25		case PushKindPush:
26			return "推"
27		case PushKindBoo:
28			return "噓"
29		case PushKindArrow:
30			return "→"
31		}
32		return "?"
33	}
34	
35	type Push struct {
36		ID          int64
37		ArticleID   int64
38		UserID      int64
39		UserUserID  string
40		Kind        PushKind
41		Body        string
42		CreatedAt   time.Time
43	}
44	
45	type PushRepo struct{ s *Store }
46	
47	const pushColumns = `id, article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body, created_at`
48	
49	func scanPush(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Push, error) {
50		p := &Push{}
51		var kind string
52		if err := row.Scan(&p.ID, &p.ArticleID, &p.UserID, &p.UserUserID, &kind, &p.Body, &p.CreatedAt); err != nil {
53			return nil, err
54		}
55		p.Kind = PushKind(kind)
56		return p, nil
57	}
58	
59	func (r *PushRepo) ListByArticle(ctx context.Context, articleID int64) ([]*Push, error) {
60		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
61			`SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE article_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, articleID,
62		)
63		if err != nil {
64			return nil, err
65		}
66		defer rows.Close()
67		var out []*Push
68		for rows.Next() {
69			p, err := scanPush(rows)
70			if err != nil {
71				return nil, err
72			}
73			out = append(out, p)
74		}
75		return out, rows.Err()
76	}
77	
78	// scoreDelta returns the recommend_score contribution of a push of the
79	// given kind. Positive on add; negate on delete. Centralized so Create
80	// and Delete agree on the algebra.
81	func scoreDelta(kind PushKind) (int, error) {
82		switch kind {
83		case PushKindPush:
84			return 1, nil
85		case PushKindBoo:
86			return -1, nil
87		case PushKindArrow:
88			return 0, nil
89		}
90		return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid push kind: %q", kind)
91	}
92	
93	// Create inserts a push and updates the cached recommend_score on the
94	// article (push: +1, boo: -1, arrow: 0). Both writes happen under the
95	// store-level writeMu so they're effectively atomic from the app's view;
96	// SQLite's WAL gives us read-consistency for the recompute.
97	func (r *PushRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, articleID, userID int64, userUserID string, kind PushKind, body string) (*Push, error) {
98		delta, err := scoreDelta(kind)
99		if err != nil {
100			return nil, err
101		}
102	
103		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
104		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
105		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
106		if err != nil {
107			return nil, err
108		}
109		defer tx.Rollback()
110	
111		res, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
112			`INSERT INTO pushes (article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
113			articleID, userID, userUserID, string(kind), body,
114		)
115		if err != nil {
116			return nil, err
117		}
118		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
119		if err != nil {
120			return nil, err
121		}
122		if delta != 0 {
123			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
124				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score + ? WHERE id = ?`,
125				delta, articleID,
126			); err != nil {
127				return nil, err
128			}
129		}
130		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
131			return nil, err
132		}
133	
134		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, id)
135		return scanPush(row)
136	}
137	
138	// Delete hard-deletes a push and reverts its score contribution on the
139	// parent article. Permission: requester must be the push author OR have
140	// a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrPermissionDenied / ErrPushNotFound.
141	// Mirrors Create's writeMu+tx discipline so concurrent deletes can't
142	// double-revert the score.
143	func (r *PushRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, pushID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
144		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
145		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
146		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
147		if err != nil {
148			return err
149		}
150		defer tx.Rollback()
151	
152		var authorID, articleID int64
153		var kindStr string
154		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
155			`SELECT user_id, article_id, kind FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID,
156		).Scan(&authorID, &articleID, &kindStr)
157		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
158			return ErrPushNotFound
159		}
160		if err != nil {
161			return err
162		}
163		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
164			return ErrPermissionDenied
165		}
166		delta, err := scoreDelta(PushKind(kindStr))
167		if err != nil {
168			return err
169		}
170		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID); err != nil {
171			return err
172		}
173		if delta != 0 {
174			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
175				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score - ? WHERE id = ?`,
176				delta, articleID,
177			); err != nil {
178				return err
179			}
180		}
181		return tx.Commit()
182	}
183
```

295	// updateDeleteConfirm handles y/n while the confirm overlay is up.
296	// y: dispatch the delete; if targeting a push, refresh the push list
297	// and the article (for the score). If targeting the article, navigate
298	// back to the board view.
299	// n / esc: cancel.
300	func (m articleViewModel) updateDeleteConfirm(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
301		switch msg.String() {
302		case "y", "Y":
303			if m.article == nil || m.deps.User == nil {
304				m.pendingDelete = false
305				return m, nil
306			}
307			ctx := context.Background()
308			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
309				pushID := m.pushes[m.pushCursor].ID
310				if err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Delete(ctx, pushID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
311					m.err = err.Error()
312					m.pendingDelete = false
313					return m, nil
314				}
315				// Refresh the push list and re-fetch the article so its
316				// recommend_score reflects the reverted delta.
317				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
318					m.pushes = pushes
319				}
320				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
321					m.article = a
322				}
323				// Clamp the cursor so the next D / p continues from a valid index.
324				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
325					m.pushCursor = -1
326				}
327				m.pendingDelete = false
328				return m, nil
329			}
330			if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Delete(ctx, m.article.ID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
331				m.err = err.Error()
332				m.pendingDelete = false
333				return m, nil
334			}
335			boardID := m.article.BoardID
336			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
337		case "n", "N", "esc":
338			m.pendingDelete = false
339			return m, nil
340		}
341		return m, nil
342	}

- `SetPinned` is the template for the new mod-only article-state mutator (mod+ only, doesn't admit author)

- Push kinds are `'push'`/`'boo'`/`'arrow'` enforced by a SQLite CHECK; the gate would mirror that style

Two real design forks worth confirming with you before I commit the plan.

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**How should the two restrictions (lock comments / arrows-only) be modeled in the schema?**

Options:
- **Single 3-way enum (Recommended)** - One column comments_mode in {open, arrows_only, locked}. Mutually exclusive states; locked supersedes arrows-only. Cleaner scan, single picker UI: M opens picker → 1/2/3 chooses. Easy to extend later (mods_only, etc.).
- **Two booleans (lock + arrows_only)** - Two columns comments_locked + comments_arrows_only. Each toggled by its own key (e.g. L for lock, A for arrows-only). Two keystrokes to learn; need a precedence rule when both set (locked wins).

**Who should be allowed to change the comments mode on an article?**

Options:
- **Mod+ only (Recommended)** - Mirrors SetPinned exactly: only role >= mod can change it; the original author cannot self-lock. Matches your phrasing "admin/mod 應該要可以..." and PTT BM convention for 板規/公告.
- **Mod+ AND the article author** - Mirrors edit/delete: author can also lock comments on their own thread. Useful if regular users post FAQs and want to freeze discussion themselves.

**Where should the lock / arrows-only state be visible to users?**

Options:
- **Both list and article view (Recommended)** - Article list gets a [鎖]/[箭] badge alongside [M] (board view). Article view shows a header badge + help-line hint. Users see the constraint before opening or pressing +/-/=. Adds minor render churn in board view on broadcast.
- **Article view only** - Smaller scope: badge only inside the article. Users discover the lock by trying to push and getting rejected. Faster to implement; list rendering untouched.


**Answer:** Single 3-way enum (Recommended), Mod+ only (Recommended), Both list and article view (Recommended)
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# Admin/Mod: Lock Comments / Arrows-Only on Articles

## Context

PTT-style boards routinely have 板規 / 公告 articles where the BM wants to **stop discussion** entirely, or **prevent 推/噓 voting** while still allowing neutral 箭頭 follow-ups. Today the BBS has no per-article moderation control over comments — anyone non-guest can push/boo/arrow on anything.

This plan adds a single article-level `comments_mode` state, settable by mod+, that gates the `+`/`-`/`=` push flow and is broadcast live to every session viewing the article or its board. It mirrors the existing `pinned_at` moderation pattern (mod-only, not author-admitted) so the surface stays internally consistent.

## Decisions (locked with the user)

- **Schema shape**: single 3-way enum `comments_mode ∈ {open, arrows_only, locked}`. Mutually exclusive; `locked` strictly stronger than `arrows_only`.
- **Permission**: `Role.AtLeast(RoleMod)` only. Author of the article cannot self-lock — mirrors `ArticleRepo.SetPinned` (`internal/store/articles.go:180`), NOT `Update`/`Delete`.
- **Display scope**: badge in both the article list (`screen_board_view.go`) and the article view header (`screen_article_view.go`).
- **Live broadcast**: yes — new `ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg{BoardID, ArticleID, Mode}` mirroring `ArticlePinChangedMsg`; receivers re-fetch from DB (DB is source of truth).
- **Defense in depth**: gate three layers — UI early (`openPush`), UI late (`updatePushInput` Enter), and store (`PushRepo.Create` inside the existing tx). The store gate is non-negotiable because multiple sessions can race against a mode change.

## State semantics

| Mode          | `+` (push) | `-` (boo) | `=` (arrow) |
|---------------|:---------:|:---------:|:-----------:|
| `open`        | ✅        | ✅        | ✅          |
| `arrows_only` | ❌        | ❌        | ✅          |
| `locked`      | ❌        | ❌        | ❌          |

Existing 推/噓 pushes on an article are **not** retroactively deleted when mode changes — only new pushes are blocked. `recommend_score` is therefore preserved across mode flips. Push **deletion** by author/mod is unaffected by mode (mods can always clean up).

No index — column is only read inline with article rows. Follows existing `0007_articles_pinned_at.sql` style.

- `TestPushes_Create_RejectsPushAndBooWhenArrowsOnly` — `comments_mode='arrows_only'`: `PushKindPush` and `PushKindBoo` → `ErrCommentsArrowsOnly`; `PushKindArrow` succeeds and `recommend_score` stays at 0.
- `TestPushes_Create_NoRegressionWhenOpen` — sanity: existing tests still pass; the `comments_mode` SELECT inside the tx doesn't break the concurrent-score canary `TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`.

### `internal/tui/screen_article_view_test.go`

- `TestArticleView_M_OpensPickerForMod` — mod presses `M`, model goes into `pickingCommentsMode=true`; non-mod presses `M`, no state change (silent no-op, consistent with `M` on board view).
- `TestArticleView_PickerSelectionsCallStore` — table-driven over `{"1": open, "2": arrows_only, "3": locked}`: after the keystroke, `Articles().GetByID` shows the new mode AND the picker closes.
- `TestArticleView_PickerEscCancels` — picker open → `esc` → picker closed, no DB write.
- `TestArticleView_PressPlusOnLockedShowsError` — set article locked, press `+` → `m.err = "本文已鎖定留言"`, `m.pushing == false` (input never opens).
- `TestArticleView_PressMinusOnArrowsOnlyShowsError` — arrows-only, press `-` → blocked; press `=` → input opens.
- `TestArticleView_HandlesArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg` — send `ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg{ArticleID: m.article.ID, Mode: "locked"}`, model re-fetches and now blocks `+`. Mismatched `ArticleID` → no-op (parity with `PushAddedMsg` test at `screen_article_view_test.go:632`).
- Mod broadcast end-to-end: after picker action, the `fakeSender` in tests records the `ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg` with correct `BoardID/ArticleID/Mode`.

### `internal/tui/screen_board_view_test.go`

- `TestBoardView_RendersLockBadgeOnLockedArticle` — seed an article with `comments_mode='locked'`, render board view, `[鎖]` appears in the row.
- `TestBoardView_RendersArrowBadgeOnArrowsOnly` — analogous.
- `TestBoardView_StacksPinAndLockBadges` — both pinned AND locked → `[M][鎖] ...` prefix.
- `TestBoardView_HandlesArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg` — send the msg with matching `BoardID`, `Articles().ListByBoard` is re-issued and the badge updates; mismatched `BoardID` → no refetch.

## Verification (end-to-end)

1. **DB layer**: `make db-reset && make test-race` — all new + existing tests pass; `TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity` still green (the new `SELECT comments_mode` inside the tx must not break the 50-goroutine canary).
2. **Migration**: `rm data/bbs.db* && make run` — fresh boot applies through `0008`. `sqlite3 data/bbs.db ".schema articles"` shows the new column with default `'open'`.
3. **Manual two-session smoke**:
   - Terminal A: `ssh admin@localhost -p 2222` (rotate password if first run).
   - Terminal B: `ssh alice@localhost -p 2222` (regular user).
   - Both navigate to `Welcome` board → open the same article.
   - In A: press `M` → see the picker → press `2`. Header in A shows `[箭] 僅開放箭頭`. Within ~1s B's article-view header updates without a manual refresh; B's board list (after going back) shows `[箭]` prefix.
   - In B: press `+` → "本文僅開放箭頭留言" toast, input never opens. Press `=` → input opens, submit a comment, push appears (it's an arrow, score unchanged).
   - In A: press `M` → `3`. Both A and B now show `[鎖]`. B presses `=` → "本文已鎖定留言".
   - In A: press `M` → `1` (back to open). All gates lift in B without reconnect.
   - As alice (regular user) in A: open any article, press `M` → silent no-op (no picker, no error toast).
4. **Defense-in-depth**: open two A-sessions as admin; one keeps the article in open mode (its model is stale because A1 hasn't re-fetched after A2's flip); A1 presses `+` → server returns `ErrCommentsLocked`, the toast surfaces it, the model heals on next view change. Confirms the store-side gate is doing real work.

## Out of scope (explicit non-goals)

- Per-board default mode (e.g., a board where all new articles default to `arrows_only`). Add later only if requested — extend `boards.attr` or add a column then.
- Time-bounded locks (auto-unlock after N hours). Trivial follow-up via a sweep job; not now.
- Retroactively deleting existing 推/噓 when switching to `arrows_only`/`locked`. Existing pushes and `recommend_score` stay frozen — clean and surprises nobody.
- Audit log of who flipped the mode and when. None of the existing moderation actions (pin, banner edit, role promotion) log either; out of scope until a broader audit story.
- Per-board moderator (`boards.bm`) enforcement. The `bm` column remains a denormalized legacy field; gate stays at the global `Role`.

## Approved Plan:
# Admin/Mod: Lock Comments / Arrows-Only on Articles

## Context

PTT-style boards routinely have 板規 / 公告 articles where the BM wants to **stop discussion** entirely, or **prevent 推/噓 voting** while still allowing neutral 箭頭 follow-ups. Today the BBS has no per-article moderation control over comments — anyone non-guest can push/boo/arrow on anything.

This plan adds a single article-level `comments_mode` state, settable by mod+, that gates the `+`/`-`/`=` push flow and is broadcast live to every session viewing the article or its board. It mirrors the existing `pinned_at` moderation pattern (mod-only, not author-admitted) so the surface stays internally consistent.

## Decisions (locked with the user)

- **Schema shape**: single 3-way enum `comments_mode ∈ {open, arrows_only, locked}`. Mutually exclusive; `locked` strictly stronger than `arrows_only`.
- **Permission**: `Role.AtLeast(RoleMod)` only. Author of the article cannot self-lock — mirrors `ArticleRepo.SetPinned` (`internal/store/articles.go:180`), NOT `Update`/`Delete`.
- **Display scope**: badge in both the article list (`screen_board_view.go`) and the article view header (`screen_article_view.go`).
- **Live broadcast**: yes — new `ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg{BoardID, ArticleID, Mode}` mirroring `ArticlePinChangedMsg`; receivers re-fetch from DB (DB is source of truth).
- **Defense in depth**: gate three layers — UI early (`openPush`), UI late (`updatePushInput` Enter), and store (`PushRepo.Create` inside the existing tx). The store gate is non-negotiable because multiple sessions can race against a mode change.

## State semantics

| Mode          | `+` (push) | `-` (boo) | `=` (arrow) |
|---------------|:---------:|:---------:|:-----------:|
| `open`        | ✅        | ✅        | ✅          |
| `arrows_only` | ❌        | ❌        | ✅          |
| `locked`      | ❌        | ❌        | ❌          |

Existing 推/噓 pushes on an article are **not** retroactively deleted when mode changes — only new pushes are blocked. `recommend_score` is therefore preserved across mode flips. Push **deletion** by author/mod is unaffected by mode (mods can always clean up).

## Files to add

- **`internal/store/migrations/0008_articles_comments_mode.sql`** — `ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN comments_mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open' CHECK (comments_mode IN ('open','arrows_only','locked'))`. No index — column is only read inline with article rows. Follows existing `0007_articles_pinned_at.sql` style.
